subject: Physics
title: Wireless Charging
description: A quick win: understand Wireless Charging.

hook.text: Set your phone on a pad and it charges—no cable. The secret is electromagnetic induction, Faraday's discovery.

buildup.text: A coil in the charging pad carries alternating current, creating a changing magnetic field.

discovery.text: That changing field induces current in a coil inside the phone—transferring energy across the air gap.

twist.text: Nikola Tesla dreamed of wireless power transmission in the 1890s. It took over a century to reach your pocket.

climax.text: New resonant systems can charge devices at a distance. Rooms that power everything inside are being developed.

punchline.text: Faraday's 1831 discovery charges your phone in 2024. No wires needed.

quiz.question: What physical principle makes wireless charging work?
options: Electromagnetic induction—a changing magnetic field induces current in a nearby coil
Electrostatic attraction between the pad and phone
Radio waves carrying energy to the phone
